ambiguity_notes: Primary legislative framework; all other instruments operate within it
Section 38(6) establishes development plan primacy subject to material considerations

legal_basis: Secretary of State direction under TCPA 1990
ambiguity_notes: Material consideration of significant weight; overrides development plan where plan is absent/outdated
Guidance on NPPF application; not statutory but given weight in appeals

Development plan document; attracts s38(6) presumption in favour unless material considerations indicate otherwise. Note: pre-2024 NPPF adoption; policy conflicts with current NPPF paras on grey belt and housing delivery test require careful weighing
Not a development plan document; material consideration only. Lower weight than adopted policies.

Development plan; relatively recent (2020 adoption). Oxford's constrained land supply creates frequent tension between NPPF housing delivery test obligations and local policies protecting green belt and settlement character
SPD; material consideration only. Amplifies adopted local plan design policies. Key ambiguity: where SPD uses different wording to local plan policy, the local plan policy prevails
Adopted plan; under transition to Greater Cambridge Local Plan. During transition, existing plan retains full weight unless specific policy directly contradicts the emerging plan
Emerging plan; weight depends on stage of preparation and degree of unresolved objections. LURA 2023 transitional arrangements apply. Major source of ISSUE-005 and ISSUE-008
Adopted 2016; approaching 10-year review point. Increasing tension with current NPPF housing policies, particularly on settlement hierarchy and affordable housing viability

Additional DPD layer; introduces renewable energy and climate policies that interact with main local plan. Where conflict arises between the two DPDs, the later document (2023) takes precedence on climate matters
London Plan is part of the statutory development plan for all London boroughs under the GLA Act 1999. It takes precedence over borough-level local plans where they conflict on strategic matters

Borough local plan; subject to London Plan conformity. Where Newham local plan policies conflict with London Plan, London Plan prevails on strategic matters. Key tension: density, affordable housing, and design policies

instrument_title: Kent Minerals and Waste Local Plan 2024-2039
ambiguity_notes: County minerals and waste DPD; operates alongside district local plans. District LPAs must take account of county plan when determining non-county-matter applications near minerals/waste safeguarding zones

instrument_title: Leeds Core Strategy and Site Allocations Plan
ambiguity_notes: Adopted DPD; Leeds is a metropolitan district so no separate county tier. West Yorkshire Combined Authority spatial framework emerging as a sub-regional layer. Key tension: housing delivery rate vs infrastructure capacity

Adopted plan approaching 10-year age; new local plan in preparation. West of England Combined Authority spatial strategy provides sub-regional context. Key tension: net zero retrofit requirements vs permitted development rights

instrument_title: Guildford Local Plan Strategy and Sites
ambiguity_notes: Recently adopted plan; significant green belt release contested at examination. Surrey County Council provides county minerals/waste and highways tier. Key ambiguity: where green belt boundaries were released by inspector modifications, development plan and NPPF green belt policy interact in complex ways

instrument_title: Newcastle Core Strategy and Urban Core Plan
ambiguity_notes: Adopted plan; North East Combined Authority spatial framework emerging. Key tension: regeneration priority areas vs heritage conservation designations. No current five-year housing land supply issues but delivery rate contested

National park local plan; national park statutory purposes and duty (PA 1949 and EA 1995) sit above s.38(6) PCPA 2004 in precedence for national park-specific matters. Key ambiguity: housing need obligations under NPPF interact with national park purposes in contested ways

Unitary authority; minerals and waste policies integrated in main plan rather than a separate county DPD. Offshore wind and energy infrastructure decisions interact with county plan policies. North East Combined Authority emerging spatial strategy adds sub-regional layer

Unitary authority; Hampshire County Council provides county context but not a planning authority for Southampton. Cross-boundary housing market with Test Valley and Eastleigh. Key tension: coastal change policy vs housing delivery targets near waterfront zones

Unitary authority; Leicestershire County Council is not the planning authority for Leicester. Leicester and Leicestershire Strategic Growth Plan provides sub-regional context. Key ambiguity: heritage conservation area policies vs urban intensification requirements

Shared emerging local plan with South Cambridgeshire. Cambridge City existing policies (2018 Local Plan) remain in force during transition. Key tension: highly constrained city with housing need overspill into South Cambridgeshire and emerging plan weight disputes

Ageing adopted plan; Medway Local Plan 2040 in preparation. Thames Estuary 2050 Growth Commission provides strategic context. Key ambiguity: ageing plan given reduced weight under NPPF housing delivery test; tilted balance frequently engaged
Adopted DPD; North East Combined Authority emerging spatial framework adds sub-regional context. Key tension: regeneration priority versus coastal change management policies
Adopted 2015; approaching 10-year review. Large rural geography with dispersed settlement hierarchy creates complex policy application. Military safeguarding zones add constraint layer

instrument_title: Brighton and Hove City Plan Part Two
ambiguity_notes: Recently adopted DPD; supplements Part One strategic policies. Key tension: acute housing pressure against heritage conservation area density and coastal constraints

Ageing adopted plan; new local plan under examination. South Yorkshire Mayoral Combined Authority provides sub-regional context. Key ambiguity: green belt review in emerging plan creates weight disputes during transition

Designated landscape local plan; Broads Act 2009 statutory purposes sit alongside s.38(6) PCPA 2004. Key tension: visitor development and navigation interests versus ecological sensitivity and flood risk
Adopted unitary plan; minerals and waste policies integrated. Key tension: former coalfield remediation and employment land pressure versus environmental protection in post-industrial areas

instrument_title: Plymouth and South West Devon Joint Local Plan
ambiguity_notes: Joint plan with South Hams and West Devon; cross-boundary housing market and naval city constraints. Key tension: waterfront regeneration versus military safeguarding and coastal flood risk

Compact urban authority; recently adopted plan. Thames Valley Berkshire LEP provides sub-regional economic context. Key tension: transport capacity constraints versus high-growth corridor housing delivery targets
Adopted DPD supplementing Aligned Core Strategy. East Midlands Combined Authority emerging. Key tension: heritage sensitivity in city centre versus urban intensification requirements

Ageing adopted plan; Places for Everyone joint plan emerging as sub-regional layer. Greater Manchester Combined Authority spatial framework adds strategic context. Key ambiguity: green belt and transport corridor pressure with cross-boundary housing implications
